In Progress

The work doesn't wait until it's finished to mean something.

UX Audit + Redesign

Complete ✓

A full audit of dark patterns in TikTok, arguing that intentional design and business success are not mutually exclusive. User interviews, behavioral mapping, a cog sci literature review, and a redesign proposing a more ethical alternative.

100% — published

View the audit ↗

Video Essay Series

Pre-production

An ongoing series on how invisible systems shape cognition and behavior. Topics: why you can't read anymore, the variable reward machine, ADHD and smartphones, why boredom is a skill. Motion graphics, research-backed, designed to be uncomfortable in the right way.

20% — scripts and research underway

Background

About

I like to think of myself as a designer who tells stories with visuals, data, and just the right amount of disruption.

My graphic design work lives in a collection called Too LOUD to IGNORE!! A body of work that represents my artist statement in full. My latest piece, In Your Face!, is a feminist poster designed for exposition that leans fully into my artistic voice: intense and unafraid of causing discomfort.

I recently completed a UX audit and redesign of TikTok that brings my cognitive science background to bear on the attention economy. Now I'm developing a video essay series on how invisible systems shape behavior. The throughline in everything I make is the same: what does this do to the person experiencing it?
